raw_themes.name: Markdown + CLAUDE.md as lightweight RAG alternative
raw_themes.short_desc: File structure + CLAUDE.md navigation replaces vector DB/embeddings for solo/small-team scale.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: you don't need vector databases, embeddings, or graph RAG if your scale is "one person or a small team."

raw_themes.name: Scale threshold drives retrieval strategy
raw_themes.short_desc: Hundreds of docs = markdown + LLM context; thousands = need real retrieval infrastructure.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: when you hit thousands of documents, you need real retrieval. Below that, file structure is enough.

raw_themes.name: CLAUDE.md-as-controller validates SoNash approach
raw_themes.short_desc: Teaching the LLM to traverse the file system is a generalizable pattern, not a Karpathy hack.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The CLAUDE.md-as-controller pattern validates our approach.

raw_themes.name: Start-simple philosophy / graduate when needed
raw_themes.short_desc: Incremental evolution from markdown → validated schema → SQLite index is the correct path.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The "start simple" philosophy is exactly right.

raw_themes.name: Simplicity gap vs CAS infrastructure
raw_themes.short_desc: 5-min Obsidian setup vs CAS session-long ramp — suggests a quick-start mode for /analyze.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: A new user could set up the Obsidian system in 5 minutes. Our CAS took an entire session to build

raw_candidates.name: Obsidian raw→wiki pipeline pattern
raw_candidates.description: Raw folder staging → LLM-generated wiki → master index. Lightweight alternative to vector RAG.

raw_candidates.name: CLAUDE.md as knowledge traversal controller
raw_candidates.description: CLAUDE.md instructs LLM how to navigate file structure, replacing vector retrieval with path-based lookup.

raw_candidates.name: Obsidian Web Clipper as ingestion tool
raw_candidates.description: Chrome extension for web→markdown ingestion directly to raw folder.

raw_candidates.name: Scale threshold decision framework
raw_candidates.description: Solo/small team <thousands docs = structured markdown sufficient. Millions = proper RAG. Start simple.

raw_candidates.name: Overcomplicating knowledge retrieval
raw_candidates.description: Jumping to vector DBs and embeddings before validating that structured markdown + LLM context works at your scale.

youtube-oszdfnqmgrw.relevance_to_home: Directly validates SoNash's existing .research/ pipeline and CLAUDE.md-as-controller pattern used for JASON-OS skill routing. The CAS is the 'mature version' of this approach; the main home-relevant takeaway is a potential quick-start mode for /analyze to close the onboarding gap.

raw_themes.name: Multi-format composable extraction pipeline
raw_themes.short_desc: Chain-of-Responsibility with reader/processor/writer handlers normalizes messy inputs to a shared flow.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: readers normalize inputs, processors transform content, writers push results somewhere. Chain of Responsibility means you snap handlers together like Lego bricks.

raw_themes.name: AST-based zero-config handler auto-discovery
raw_themes.short_desc: Factory walks handler tree, parses Python ASTs, registers AbstractHandler subclasses by class name.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The factory pattern with AST-based auto-discovery is the most genuinely clever thing here.

raw_themes.name: PII tokenize/untokenize round-trip for LLM privacy
raw_themes.short_desc: Replace PII with T1/T2/T3 tokens before LLM, instruct model to use tokens as names, untokenize after.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Tokenize PII before sending to the LLM (T1, T2, T3), instruct the model to use tokens as names, untokenize after.

raw_themes.name: Local-first extraction over AWS-coupled stacks
raw_themes.short_desc: Whisper/Tesseract/spaCy local alternatives vs Transcribe/Textract/Comprehend vendor lock-in and billing complexity.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: For JASON-OS's portable-tools vision, extraction should use local-first alternatives where possible: Whisper instead of Transcribe

raw_themes.name: YouTube → audio → transcript lifecycle
raw_themes.short_desc: pytubefix download + moviepy audio extract + Transcribe/Whisper async lifecycle in ~30 lines per handler.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Uses `pytubefix` for download + `moviepy` for audio extraction to MP3. This is the exact YouTube content pipeline T27 needs.

raw_themes.name: Model-agnostic invocation via JSONPath
raw_themes.short_desc: Env-driven JSONPath request/response mapping lets same code invoke Claude, Titan, or any Bedrock model.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Same invocation code works for Claude, Titan, or any Bedrock model.

raw_candidates.name: YouTube audio extraction pipeline
raw_candidates.description: pytubefix download + moviepy audio extraction to MP3. Full lifecycle in ~30 lines. Reference for T27 YouTube content extraction.

raw_candidates.name: Amazon Transcribe async job lifecycle
raw_candidates.description: Start transcription job -> poll -> fetch transcript from S3 -> cleanup. Shows speaker labels, language config, S3 output routing.

raw_candidates.name: PII tokenize/untokenize round-trip pattern
raw_candidates.description: Comprehend PII detection -> token replacement (T1/T2/T3) -> token map persistence -> LLM processing with tokens -> untokenization. Preserves context while anonymizing.

raw_candidates.name: Chain of Responsibility + Factory handler composition
raw_candidates.description: Reader/processor/writer handler taxonomy with set_next() chaining. Factory with lazy import and caching. Clean pipeline composition model.

raw_candidates.name: AST-based handler auto-discovery
raw_candidates.description: HandlerFactory.discover_handlers walks handler tree, parses Python ASTs to find AbstractHandler subclasses, registers for lazy import. Zero-config extension model.

raw_candidates.name: JSONPath model-agnostic invocation config
raw_candidates.description: Environment-driven model request/response mapping via JSONPath expressions. Same code invokes Claude v2, Claude 3, or Titan by changing env vars.

raw_candidates.name: pytubefix YouTube download + caption library
raw_candidates.description: 1.5K stars, MIT, actively maintained. Built-in caption/SRT extraction may skip Whisper for captioned videos; audio-only download; playlist/channel enumeration; AsyncYouTube; OAuth.

raw_candidates.name: AWS-coupled extraction anti-pattern
raw_candidates.description: 5+ AWS service dependencies (Transcribe, Textract, Comprehend, Bedrock, S3, Rekognition) create vendor lock-in, billing complexity, and failure surface. Prefer local-first alternatives (Whisper, Tesseract, spaCy) for portable tools.

raw_candidates.name: Blocking poll anti-pattern
raw_candidates.description: Transcribe handler polls with time.sleep(30) in a blocking while loop. Acceptable for CLI, but must not be copied into agent pipelines. Use async callbacks or event-driven patterns instead.

bedrock-summarize-audio-video-text.relevance_to_home: High relevance via two axes: (1) PII tokenize/untokenize pattern is directly applicable to SoNash journal entries (sobriety-community data is extremely sensitive — names, substances, sponsors, meetings); (2) handler taxonomy + AST auto-discovery is a meta-tooling reference for JASON-OS Domain 02a (MCP/skill registration).

raw_themes.name: Output representation matters as much as parsing
raw_themes.short_desc: DoclingDocument tree (body/furniture, hierarchical sections, JSON pointers) vs flat Element lists.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: the output representation matters as much as the parsing

raw_themes.name: Backend + Pipeline + DoclingDocument three-layer separation
raw_themes.short_desc: Parsing → processing → output cleanly separated; one backend per format, pipelines orchestrate, DoclingDocument unified.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: This three-layer separation (parsing → processing → output) is exactly the kind of clean architecture that survives scaling.

raw_themes.name: Plugin system via pluggy (setuptools entrypoints)
raw_themes.short_desc: Third-party OCR/layout/table model registration without forking. Most sophisticated extension mechanism in the space.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The plugin system via pluggy is the most sophisticated extension mechanism in the document processing space.

raw_themes.name: Unified output across source types (VLM + ASR + docs)
raw_themes.short_desc: GraniteDocling VLM, Whisper ASR, and classical PDF pipelines all converge to DoclingDocument.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Every source type, same output format. This is the unified extraction vision T28 is chasing.

raw_themes.name: MCP-first delegation over building extractors
raw_themes.short_desc: docling-mcp exposes conversion as MCP tool — T28 should delegate rather than reimplement.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Use docling as T28's document extraction backend instead of building one.

raw_themes.name: Thread-safe pipeline with per-run isolation
raw_themes.short_desc: Per-run identifiers, bounded queues with back-pressure, minimal shared state — production engineering.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: per-run isolation, deterministic run identifiers, bounded queues with back-pressure, and minimal shared state.

raw_themes.name: Premature multi-package split risk
raw_themes.short_desc: 5 packages makes sense for an ecosystem with real consumers; not for a single-user orchestration system.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Start monolithic, split only when you have external consumers

raw_candidates.name: DoclingDocument tree structure
raw_candidates.description: Pydantic tree-structured unified document representation. Body/furniture separation, reading order via tree, JSON pointer cross-refs. T28 output schema design reference.

raw_candidates.name: Backend + Pipeline separation pattern
raw_candidates.description: Backends handle format-specific parsing, pipelines orchestrate processing stages. T28 should separate source extraction from analysis orchestration.

raw_candidates.name: Plugin system via pluggy
raw_candidates.description: Third-party extensibility via setuptools entrypoints. OCR, layout, table models. Only needed when T28 has external consumers.

raw_candidates.name: docling-mcp as extraction backend
raw_candidates.description: MCP server wrapping docling conversion. T28 could use as document extraction backend instead of building one.

raw_candidates.name: ASR pipeline → unified output
raw_candidates.description: Whisper Turbo → DoclingDocument. Same output as PDF/DOCX. Proves the unified extraction output thesis T28 is pursuing.

raw_candidates.name: Use docling instead of building document extractors
raw_candidates.description: Docling already solves PDF, DOCX, PPTX, HTML, audio, images, LaTeX. Delegate via docling-mcp, build only what docling doesn't cover.

raw_candidates.name: Serializer hierarchy pattern
raw_candidates.description: BaseDocSerializer → per-format serializers. Per-component serializers (text, table, picture). T28 export layer reference.

raw_candidates.name: Enrichment pipeline (toggleable)
raw_candidates.description: Optional post-processing steps: code understanding, formula parsing, picture classification. Pattern for T28 analysis depth options.

raw_candidates.name: Thread-safe pipeline design
raw_candidates.description: Per-run isolation, bounded queues, back-pressure, deterministic shutdown. Production engineering reference.

raw_candidates.name: InputFormat enum (17 types)
raw_candidates.description: Compare with unstructured's FileType enum. Docling's is simpler (just string enum) but paired with FormatOption mapping.

raw_candidates.name: Don't replicate multi-package split prematurely
raw_candidates.description: 5 packages makes sense for a library ecosystem with 57K stars. T28 has one user — start monolithic.

raw_candidates.name: Don't build plugin system before you have plugins
raw_candidates.description: pluggy exists because docling has real third-party contributors. Build hardcoded extractors first, plugins later.

raw_candidates.name: Don't adopt DoclingDocument wholesale
raw_candidates.description: DoclingDocument is optimized for single-document representation. T28 needs cross-source analysis schema. Study the tree pattern, design your own.

docling.relevance_to_home: Very high for JASON-OS Domain 02a: docling-mcp is a canonical example of delegating heavy extraction to an MCP server rather than reimplementing, and docling's Backend+Pipeline separation is the cleanest architecture pattern seen for T28's extraction → analysis → synthesis vision.

raw_themes.name: Tight loop with one file + one metric + no gates
raw_themes.short_desc: Optimal agent workflow: 3 files, 1 metric, no permission gates. Everything else is noise.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: the optimal agent workflow is a tight loop with one file, one metric, and no permission gates. Everything else is noise.

raw_themes.name: 3-file architecture: immutable + editable + instructions
raw_themes.short_desc: prepare.py (immutable), train.py (agent-editable), program.md (instructions). Clean editable-zone contract.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: prepare.py is immutable infrastructure. train.py is the agent-editable surface. program.md is the instruction set.

raw_themes.name: Fixed-budget experimentation
raw_themes.short_desc: TIME_BUDGET=300 makes all experiments comparable. ~100 experiments per overnight run.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The fixed 5-minute time budget is the smartest constraint: it makes every experiment directly comparable

raw_themes.name: Simplicity criterion as taste-encoded instruction
raw_themes.short_desc: 'Removing something and getting equal or better results is a great outcome.' Research taste as agent instruction.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: A 0.001 val_bpb improvement that adds 20 lines of hacky code? Probably not worth it. A 0.001 val_bpb improvement from deleting code? Definitely keep.

raw_themes.name: Protocol vs workflow design space
raw_themes.short_desc: program.md (protocol: one agent, one metric, loop) vs SKILL.md (workflow: multi-agent, multi-phase, convergence).
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Different tools for different constraint spaces.

raw_themes.name: Hidden multi-agent architecture via .gitignore
raw_themes.short_desc: worktrees/, queue/, generated CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md tell a story of parallel-experiment infra not in public repo.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The public repo is the single-agent baseline; the multi-agent extension exists but isn't shared.

raw_themes.name: Simplicity discipline haunts sophisticated systems
raw_themes.short_desc: The uncomfortable question for every piece of complexity: what does it buy that simpler wouldn't?
raw_themes.evidence_quote: for each piece of complexity, can you articulate what it buys you that a simpler approach wouldn't?

raw_candidates.name: program.md Agent Instruction Pattern
raw_candidates.description: 114-line autonomous research protocol: setup, experiment loop, keep/discard, crash recovery, NEVER STOP, simplicity criterion, TSV logging. Compare against SKILL.md.

raw_candidates.name: Fixed-Budget Experimentation
raw_candidates.description: Fixed 5-min time budget makes all experiments directly comparable. ~12/hour, ~100 overnight. Generalizable to any time-bounded agent work.

raw_candidates.name: 3-File Architecture (Immutable/Editable/Instructions)
raw_candidates.description: prepare.py (don't touch) + train.py (agent edits) + program.md (human edits). Clean editable-zone contract.

raw_candidates.name: Autonomous Crash Recovery Protocol
raw_candidates.description: Read stack trace, attempt fix, revert if unfixable. Simpler than checkpoint+resume for low-cost tasks.

raw_candidates.name: Results TSV Logging Convention
raw_candidates.description: commit/val_bpb/memory_gb/status/description. Already have JSONL equivalent.

raw_candidates.name: Simplicity criterion for JASON-OS
raw_candidates.description: 'Removing something and getting equal or better results is a great outcome.' Apply to every new skill, agent, or infrastructure addition. The uncomfortable question: for each piece of complexity, what does it buy?

raw_candidates.name: Autonomy spectrum design
raw_candidates.description: NEVER STOP (autoresearch) vs explicit approval gates (CLAUDE.md Guardrail #2). JASON-OS needs to find the right point: autonomous within phases, gated between phases.

raw_candidates.name: Protocol vs workflow design space
raw_candidates.description: program.md (protocol: one agent, one metric, infinite loop) vs SKILL.md (workflow: multi-agent, multi-phase, convergence). Different tools for different constraint spaces.

raw_candidates.name: analysis.ipynb — Experiment Results Methodology
raw_candidates.description: Framework for analyzing agent experiment history. Load results.tsv, compute keep/discard/crash rates, plot val_bpb with running minimum, label kept experiments, rank improvements by delta magnitude.

raw_candidates.name: Hidden Multi-Agent Architecture (.gitignore)
raw_candidates.description: worktrees/ + queue/ + generated CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md. Multi-agent experiment infrastructure not in public repo. Mirrors home worktree usage.

raw_candidates.name: No-license-on-purpose trap
raw_candidates.description: Karpathy can get away with no license because he's Karpathy. 9,611 forks in legal ambiguity. Don't follow this example for JASON-OS.

raw_candidates.name: Single-metric optimization trap
raw_candidates.description: val_bpb is clean because the problem has one unambiguous metric. Most real work has competing metrics. Don't flatten multi-objective problems into one number to look like autoresearch.

karpathy-autoresearch.relevance_to_home: High meta-tooling relevance: program.md vs SKILL.md directly challenges JASON-OS skill-format assumptions, and the simplicity criterion is aimed at the home repo's 72-skill/38-agent/8,490-debt-item scale. The 3-file architecture maps cleanly to editable-zone design for future JASON-OS portability.

raw_themes.name: Structural validation prevents format decay
raw_themes.short_desc: format.py + links.py + tests + three CI workflows enforce `API|Description|Auth|HTTPS|CORS` schema.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: This is a curated list that invested in structural integrity tooling

raw_themes.name: Validation without maintenance still stagnates
raw_themes.short_desc: Automation ensures format integrity but not content freshness or link liveness; lifecycle mgmt missing.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: format validation scripts don't prevent stagnation. The scripts ensure structural integrity of new entries, but they don't ensure entries are current

raw_themes.name: Commercial capture as stagnation mechanism
raw_themes.short_desc: Sponsor-first README (10 APILayer APIs before community content) erodes trust and signals monetization capture.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: This is the "commercial capture" stage of celebrity stagnation

raw_themes.name: Link rot as silent documentation killer
raw_themes.short_desc: Daily cron + on-push link checking is a small, transferable win for docs/SKILL_INDEX/EXTRACTIONS/MEMORY.md.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: A scheduled or on-push link checker would catch rotting references before they mislead.

raw_themes.name: SoNash-relevant API candidates surfaced
raw_themes.short_desc: Google Calendar (OAuth via Firebase), Open-Meteo (no auth, CORS), GCP Natural Language for sentiment — recovery notebook leverage.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: For a future "recovery resources" feature, health/wellness APIs are the starting point.

raw_candidates.name: Link Validation Workflow
raw_candidates.description: validate_links.yml + scripts/validate/links.py (273 lines) + tests (172 lines). Scheduled + on-push URL liveness checking using Python requests.

raw_candidates.name: Structured Catalog Format (enum columns)
raw_candidates.description: API|Description|Auth|HTTPS|CORS table format with constrained enum values. Machine-parseable and human-readable.

raw_candidates.name: Format Validation Script Pattern
raw_candidates.description: format.py (277 lines) with regex-based format enforcement, category structure validation, alphabetical ordering. Simpler version of patterns:check.

raw_candidates.name: Celebrity stagnation with infrastructure (cross-repo)
raw_candidates.description: Same trajectory as codecrafters but WITH validation scripts, CI, tests. Proves automation alone doesn't prevent stagnation — active maintenance is the key differentiator.

raw_candidates.name: Validation without maintenance trap
raw_candidates.description: Format validation scripts ensure structural integrity of new entries, but don't ensure entries are current, links are live, or categories are relevant. Missing piece: lifecycle management.

raw_candidates.name: Google Calendar API
raw_candidates.description: Direct SoNash integration candidate. OAuth via Firebase Auth. Milestones, meetings, check-ins.

raw_candidates.name: Open-Meteo Weather API (no auth)
raw_candidates.description: Zero-friction weather data for mood-weather correlation journaling. No API key, CORS enabled.

raw_candidates.name: Google Cloud Natural Language API
raw_candidates.description: Journal sentiment analysis. Same cloud ecosystem as Firebase.

raw_candidates.name: validate_links.yml workflow
raw_candidates.description: 29-line daily link checker. Port to sonash for docs/SKILL_INDEX/EXTRACTIONS link integrity.

raw_candidates.name: Sponsor-first README pattern
raw_candidates.description: 10 APILayer promoted APIs before community content. Erodes trust. If JASON-OS has partners, keep them separate from the content index.

raw_candidates.name: Validation without maintenance trap
raw_candidates.description: Format validation scripts ensure structural integrity but not content freshness. Missing piece: lifecycle management. patterns:check prevents format decay but not content decay.

public-apis_public-apis.relevance_to_home: Two concrete SoNash leverage points (Google Calendar OAuth via Firebase, GCP Natural Language for journal sentiment) plus the directly-portable validate_links.yml pattern for SKILL_INDEX/EXTRACTIONS/MEMORY link-rot detection. The 'validation without maintenance' trap is a direct warning to JASON-OS skill-audit automation.

raw_themes.name: Unified abstraction over many formats
raw_themes.short_desc: partition() hides 30+ parsers behind one call; libmagic auto-detect + typed Element output.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: the problem isn't parsing any single format — it's building a unified abstraction that makes 30+ formats look identical to downstream consumers.

raw_themes.name: Self-describing FileType registry
raw_themes.short_desc: Each enum member declares partitioner path, deps, extras, MIME types, extensions — add format = add enum member.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The FileType enum (file_utils/model.py) is a masterclass in self-describing registration.

raw_themes.name: Strategy fallback with graceful dependency checking
raw_themes.short_desc: AUTO/FAST/OCR_ONLY/HI_RES cascade; falls back when optional ML deps absent. Maps to Quick/Standard/Deep tiers.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The strategy system (AUTO/FAST/OCR_ONLY/HI_RES) with graceful dependency fallback is a mature pattern

raw_themes.name: Typed elements with provenance metadata
raw_themes.short_desc: Elements carry coordinates, data source metadata, permissions data — extraction with spatial + trust awareness.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: This isn't just "text in, text out" — it's document intelligence with spatial awareness.

raw_themes.name: Chunking is deep — not a utility
raw_themes.short_desc: 1800-line chunking/base.py handles table isolation, title semantics, token counting, cross-page chunks, header repetition.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: These are deep problems that only surface at scale.

raw_themes.name: Golden-file snapshot testing for extraction
raw_themes.short_desc: 40+ connector types tested via expected markdown comparison — regression model for T28.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: 40+ connector types tested via expected markdown output comparison. Model for T28 extraction regression testing

raw_themes.name: Over-engineered ontology is tech debt even authors admit
raw_themes.short_desc: Pydantic HTML intermediate representation adds complexity without value — author TODO says so.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The TODO comment from "Pluto" in ontology.py explicitly says OntologyElement is the only needed class

raw_candidates.name: Auto-routing via type detection
raw_candidates.description: partition() auto-detects file type via libmagic and routes to type-specific partitioner. T28 needs equivalent: detect source type (URL, repo, file) and route to right extractor.

raw_candidates.name: Self-describing FileType registry
raw_candidates.description: Enum where each member declares partitioner path, deps, extras, MIME types, extensions. Adding a format = adding one enum member. T28 source type registration pattern.

raw_candidates.name: Strategy fallback chain
raw_candidates.description: AUTO→HI_RES→OCR_ONLY→FAST with dependency checking. Maps to T28 Quick/Standard/Deep with graceful degradation when tools unavailable.

raw_candidates.name: Graceful dependency checking
raw_candidates.description: dependency_exists() checks at runtime, logs warning, falls back. T28 needs this for optional deps (Whisper, Chrome, gh CLI).

raw_candidates.name: Golden-file snapshot testing
raw_candidates.description: 40+ connector types tested via expected markdown output comparison. Model for T28 extraction regression testing.

raw_candidates.name: Element type hierarchy with metadata
raw_candidates.description: Dataclass elements with coordinates, data source provenance, permissions. Informs T28 extraction output schema design.

raw_candidates.name: Chunking with table isolation
raw_candidates.description: Tables never merge with adjacent text during chunking. Title-based semantic boundaries. Relevant for T28 analysis layer.

raw_candidates.name: Text cleaning pipeline
raw_candidates.description: Bullet normalization, ligature replacement, whitespace cleanup. Reminder that extraction output needs post-processing normalization.

raw_candidates.name: S3-backed performance benchmarking
raw_candidates.description: Benchmark results tagged with architecture/instance/git hash. Production-grade perf tracking model.

raw_candidates.name: unstructured-ingest connector ecosystem
raw_candidates.description: 40+ source connectors (Salesforce, Notion, Confluence, Jira, Slack, Discord, S3, etc.). T28 connector layer reference.

raw_candidates.name: Don't replicate ontology V2 over-engineering
raw_candidates.description: Pydantic HTML intermediate representation adds complexity without value. Team acknowledges it. T28 should go extraction→typed output directly.

raw_candidates.name: Don't build per-format parsers in TypeScript
raw_candidates.description: 30+ partitioners represent years of edge cases. T28 should delegate parsing to existing tools and focus on orchestration.

raw_candidates.name: Don't fragment extraction and analysis across repos
raw_candidates.description: Embedding moved to unstructured-ingest, fragmenting DX. Keep T28 extraction→analysis→synthesis as single coherent system.

unstructured.relevance_to_home: Single most architecturally relevant repo for T28 CAS. The FileType registry pattern and auto-routing via type detection map directly to /analyze router improvements. The strategy fallback chain generalizes Quick/Standard/Deep to handle missing optional deps gracefully — a concrete upgrade path for the CAS.

raw_themes.name: Adversarial infrastructure design
raw_themes.short_desc: Every design decision assumes the upstream (Twitter) will try to block you; session pooling, rate limit tracking, parser versioning.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Nitter understands adversarial infrastructure -- how to build a service that works against the interests of its data source.

raw_themes.name: Docker hardening as security boundary
raw_themes.short_desc: Read-only fs, CAP_DROP ALL, non-root UID 998, health checks — best Docker hardening in the corpus.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Most projects treat Docker as a packaging format; nitter treats it as a security boundary.

raw_themes.name: Parser versioning isolates blast radius of upstream API changes
raw_themes.short_desc: experimental/ directory lets new parsers develop alongside old ones — switch rather than patch.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: When the upstream changes, you switch parsers rather than patching the existing one. This isolates blast radius.

raw_themes.name: Per-endpoint preemptive rate limiting
raw_themes.short_desc: Track rate limits per endpoint with preemptive backoff — more sophisticated than post-429 handling.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: limiting before hitting 429, not after

raw_themes.name: Adversarial dependency as existential risk
raw_themes.short_desc: 12.8K stars but effectively dead — Twitter killed nitter by changing APIs. Never build on a hostile foundation.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Twitter killed nitter by changing APIs. The project has 12.8K stars but is effectively dead.

raw_themes.name: XSS + missing security headers in content renderer
raw_themes.short_desc: Karax verbatim bypasses escaping; no CSP, no X-Frame-Options, no HSTS; plaintext credentials.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The XSS vulnerability (unescaped tweet content via Karax verbatim) is a critical flaw

raw_candidates.name: Docker container hardening (RO fs + CAP_DROP ALL)
raw_candidates.description: Read-only filesystem, CAP_DROP ALL, non-root uid 998, alpine minimal, health checks. Best Docker hardening in T29 corpus.

raw_candidates.name: Session pool with per-endpoint rate limiting
raw_candidates.description: Multiple sessions rotate on rate limits. Per-session per-endpoint tracking with preemptive backoff.

raw_candidates.name: Parser versioning for unstable APIs
raw_candidates.description: Experimental module allows parallel parser development. Isolates blast radius of upstream API changes.

raw_candidates.name: ADVERSARIAL_DEPENDENCY
raw_candidates.description: Building product on scraping hostile platform. Twitter killed nitter by changing APIs. 12.8K stars, effectively dead.

raw_candidates.name: XSS_IN_CONTENT_RENDERER
raw_candidates.description: Rendering untrusted content without HTML escaping. Karax verbatim bypasses escaping.

raw_candidates.name: SECURITY_HEADERS_ABSENT
raw_candidates.description: No CSP, X-Frame-Options, HSTS in web application. Zero defense-in-depth.

zedeus-nitter.relevance_to_home: Low direct SoNash relevance (Firebase, not Docker) but the hardening reference is canonical for any future JASON-OS containerization. The adversarial-dependency anti-pattern reinforces the SoNash choice to build on Firebase (stable API) rather than scraped/unstable upstreams.

raw_themes.name: 6-pattern orchestration taxonomy
raw_themes.short_desc: Parallel Specialists, Pipeline, Swarm, Research+Implementation, Plan Approval, Coordinated Refactoring.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The 6 orchestration patterns are the most valuable synthesis

raw_themes.name: Subagent vs teammate as design tradeoff
raw_themes.short_desc: Subagents = fire-and-forget simplicity; teammates = persistent inbox messaging with lifecycle complexity.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Subagents are simpler but can't coordinate mid-task. Teammates can coordinate but add lifecycle complexity

raw_themes.name: Spawn backend comparison (in-process/tmux/iterm2)
raw_themes.short_desc: Visible-persistent vs fastest-hidden tradeoff; relevant to T5 worktree management strategy.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The spawn backend comparison (in-process vs tmux vs iterm2) is directly relevant.

raw_themes.name: Task dependency auto-unblock replaces manual sequencing
raw_themes.short_desc: Declarative blockedBy arrays with auto-unblock could replace manual sequential dispatch in deep-research.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The task dependency auto-unblock feature could replace your manual sequential dispatch if you formalize it.

raw_themes.name: Reference documentation without verification layer
raw_themes.short_desc: 82 code blocks document HOW but never WHEN or WHETHER — home convergence loops fill that gap.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: This gist's patterns dispatch agents and collect results but never verify quality.

raw_themes.name: SKILL.md as context-loadable instruction set
raw_themes.short_desc: Gist uses SKILL.md frontmatter to be loadable into Claude Code as an instruction set — same pattern as home skills.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The gist is structured as a SKILL.md file (it has the frontmatter format), which means it's designed to be loaded into Claude Code's context as an instruction set.

raw_candidates.name: 6-pattern orchestration taxonomy for AGENT_ORCHESTRATION.md
raw_candidates.description: Parallel Specialists, Pipeline, Swarm, Research+Implementation, Plan Approval, Coordinated Refactoring. Compare against existing patterns in AGENT_ORCHESTRATION.md and identify which patterns skills use implicitly but haven't named.

raw_candidates.name: Spawn backend comparison for T5 worktree management
raw_candidates.description: in-process (hidden, fastest), tmux (visible, persistent), iterm2 (macOS split). Auto-detection logic. Directly relevant to T5 worktree management and parallel CC instance strategies.

raw_candidates.name: TeammateTool 13-operation reference for agent team definitions
raw_candidates.description: Full operation lifecycle for persistent teams. Compare against .claude/teams/ definitions — are teams using all available operations? Are teams missing shutdown/cleanup protocols?

raw_candidates.name: Task dependency auto-unblock for skill pipeline design
raw_candidates.description: Declarative task dependencies via blockedBy arrays. Auto-unblock on completion. Could replace manual orchestration in multi-phase skills like deep-research and repo-synthesis.

raw_candidates.name: Subagent vs teammate decision framework
raw_candidates.description: Short-lived focused work = subagent (Task tool alone). Persistent coordination with messaging = teammate (Task + team). Clear decision criteria for when to use which.

kieranklaassen-gist-4f2aba89.relevance_to_home: High relevance for JASON-OS meta-tooling: this is the most complete reference manual for Claude Code's multi-agent system and directly surfaces gaps in AGENT_ORCHESTRATION.md (missing named patterns, unused teammate operations, manual sequencing where auto-unblock would fit).

initial_clusters.cluster_name: Multi-format extraction with unified output schema

member_themes.theme_name: Output representation matters as much as parsing
Unified output across source types (VLM + ASR + docs)

initial_clusters.short_desc: Three repos independently converge on the thesis that value is in the unified output representation + routing, not in any single format parser.

initial_clusters.cluster_name: Self-describing registries / auto-routing over explicit skill selection
initial_clusters.short_desc: Adding a format/handler should be a one-liner (enum member / file drop-in); the routing infrastructure should handle the rest. Directly applicable to /analyze router.

member_themes.theme_name: Tight loop with one file + one metric + no gates

initial_clusters.short_desc: Repeated theme across multiple source types: start minimal, delegate to existing tools, resist premature abstraction. Hits JASON-OS growth discipline directly.

member_themes.theme_name: Subagent vs teammate as design tradeoff

initial_clusters.short_desc: Strong convergence on how agents are organized: instruction-file-as-controller (CLAUDE.md/program.md/SKILL.md), editable-zone contracts, and orchestration taxonomies. JASON-OS Domain 02a fuel.

initial_clusters.cluster_name: Validation infrastructure alone does not prevent decay

member_themes.theme_name: Over-engineered ontology is tech debt even authors admit

initial_clusters.short_desc: Three angles on the same lesson: structural validation / sophisticated architecture / infrastructure hardening do not save a project from upstream or content decay. Lifecycle management is the missing layer.

XSS + missing security headers in content renderer

initial_clusters.short_desc: Privacy/security appears twice in very different forms: proactive (PII tokens before LLM, Docker hardening) and reactive (nitter's XSS + missing headers as a warning).

absence_signals.expected_because: SoNash is a recovery notebook for the sobriety community; one would expect at least one source covering journaling/recovery/meeting-reminder UX.
absence_signals.suggestion: Future /analyze runs should seed a recovery-notebook candidate list (e.g., I Am Sober app, Sober Grid, Aa.org meeting finder API) — the public-apis catalog only surfaced generic Health APIs, mostly COVID-era.

absence_signals.domain: Firebase-specific architecture / App Check / Firestore repository patterns
absence_signals.expected_because: SoNash is built on Firebase 12.10 with App Check enforced and a firestore-service.ts repository pattern; corpus includes no Firebase deep-dive.
absence_signals.suggestion: Add a dedicated Firebase/App Check source (Firebase docs, Firebase Auth + COOP/COEP pattern examples) to Wave 6 corpus.

absence_signals.expected_because: Bedrock repo explicitly flags local-first as the right direction and references a commented-out Whisper handler; corpus has no Whisper-native repo in this slice.
absence_signals.suggestion: If not covered in other slices, pursue a local-Whisper / whisper.cpp source to complement bedrock/docling ASR coverage.

absence_signals.expected_because: Home invests heavily in hook governance, pattern compliance, TDMS; corpus in this slice gives no direct external case study of hook ecosystems.
absence_signals.suggestion: Wave 6 should include one deliberately-chosen source on pre-commit ecosystems (e.g., pre-commit/pre-commit, lefthook, husky ecosystems) to test home's hook posture against external practice.

absence_signals.expected_because: Sobriety data is HIPAA/42 CFR Part 2 adjacent; PII tokenization only covers the LLM boundary, not storage/auth.
absence_signals.suggestion: Add a source on 42 CFR Part 2 / SUD data handling patterns or privacy-first journal app case studies.

notes: Slice has extremely strong convergence on unified-extraction + registry + delegation themes (docling + unstructured + bedrock), which will likely be the single biggest theme when merged across all 4 slices given T28's focus. Parent should expect heavy dedup of 'self-describing registry' and 'MCP delegation' candidates.

karpathy-autoresearch and kieranklaassen-gist are both meta-tooling sources that challenge home's skill/agent architecture from opposite directions: autoresearch says 'your system is too complex', the gist says 'your system under-uses available capabilities'. Both should land in the parent synthesis as a tension, not an averaging.

The PII tokenize/untokenize pattern from bedrock is the single most SoNash-specific practical candidate in the slice — worth elevating above its tier rating because journal entries in a sobriety app are uniquely sensitive.

zedeus-nitter's adversarial-dependency anti-pattern is worth surfacing at the slice level even though the repo itself is dead: it is a strong 'don't build on hostile foundations' signal that validates SoNash's Firebase choice and JASON-OS's caution about external MCP server reliability.

public-apis surfaced Google Calendar / Open-Meteo / GCP Natural Language as concrete SoNash feature hooks — these are content candidates that parent should preserve as they are the only slice outputs that are product-level leverage rather than meta-tooling.

Candidate 'Validation without maintenance trap' appears both as knowledge and anti-pattern in value-map.json for public-apis; I preserved both as source-authored. Parent may want to dedupe into a single entry.

youtube-oszdfnqmgrw creator-view is unusually self-referential — nearly every theme validates something SoNash already does. This is low-novelty, high-confirmation signal and should be weighted accordingly in synthesis.
All 8 sources had complete, readable artifacts — no empties encountered.
